steps.title: Identify what the question needs
steps.content: The passage sets up a specific problem: it is hard to count northern muriquis accurately, so it is hard to tell whether their population is increasing, decreasing, or stable. The blank must complete the sentence "The study may thus ______" in a way that flows logically from that counting problem.

steps.title: Eliminate the choices
steps.content:
• Choice A — cause other conservationists to adopt a new methodology for counting populations.: The passage says counting is currently difficult, not that the study will fix the method or inspire others to change theirs. This is unsupported. ✗
  • Choice B — risk making inaccurate conclusions about the effectiveness of different conservation strategies.: If population change cannot be reliably measured, the study cannot reliably tell whether any conservation strategy worked — so it risks drawing inaccurate conclusions. This follows directly from the stated problem. ✓
  • Choice C — benefit from including species beyond the northern muriqui.: The passage mentions no limitation related to focusing on one species; the problem is counting difficulty, not species scope. ✗
  • Choice D — fail to consider less-well-known conservation approaches for the northern muriqui.: The passage raises no concern about which strategies are considered — only about measuring population changes accurately. ✗
B is correct.
